recommendations.rationale: Matches the approved direction of a mobile-first responsive web MVP focused on preventive guidance rather than medical record keeping.
This is the minimum required onboarding input and the first step needed to generate value within 60 seconds.
The core MVP promise is immediate plan generation followed by a prioritized dashboard view.
The main follow-through action loop in v1 is updating item status or setting a reminder from item context.
The profile is the primary person-specific unit that drives rule-based guidance, family mode, and dashboard separation.
The generated plan is the central product artifact that turns minimal inputs into actionable preventive guidance.
Items are the actionable units users review, complete, and attach reminders to across checkups and vaccinations.
The MVP success criteria depend on getting users from first input to a populated dashboard extremely quickly.
Status updates and reminders are the simplest repeat-engagement loop that delivers ongoing value without expanding scope.
A TypeScript Express backend is simple, fast to implement, and sufficient for a modular MVP with clear domain boundaries.
Next.js with React and TypeScript supports a mobile-first responsive web app, fast iteration, and later client portability.
PostgreSQL is a reliable default for structured relational data such as accounts, profiles, plans, items, reminders, and vaccinations.
Prisma fits a lean TypeScript stack, keeps schema changes explicit, and speeds MVP delivery with strong developer ergonomics.
A modular monolith is the safest MVP choice because it preserves clean boundaries without introducing premature distributed-system complexity.
Delivering the MVP in thin end-to-end slices reduces ambiguity and keeps architecture aligned with real shipped behavior.
VS Code is the most practical default for a TypeScript-heavy web stack and supports fast team onboarding.
The brief explicitly requires generating the first health plan within 5 seconds after onboarding completion.
The product framing emphasizes minimal sensitive data, high trust, and avoiding medical-record scope, so privacy must be a first-class constraint.
